text: Trust in God, but tie your camel.
Patience is the key to relief.
The world is a bridge; cross it but do not build upon it.
A kind word can attract even the snake from his nest.
He who has health has hope, and he who has hope has everything.
The wound of words is worse than the wound of swords.
Write the bad things that are done to you in sand, but write the good things that happen to you on a piece of marble.
A book is a garden carried in the pocket.
If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.
The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.
When the student is ready, the teacher will appear.
Examine what is said, not who speaks.
He who does not travel does not know the value of men.
Knowledge is a treasure that follows its owner everywhere.
Sticks in a bundle are unbreakable.
The greatest wealth is contentment with little.
Silence is the best reply to a fool.
A man's worth is determined by his two smallest organs: his heart and his tongue.
The one who plants trees, knowing that he will never sit in their shade, has at least started to understand the meaning of life.
An army of sheep led by a lion would defeat an army of lions led by a sheep.
If you have much, give of your wealth; if you have little, give of your heart.
A wise man associating with the vicious becomes an idiot.
Four things come not back: the spoken word, the sped arrow, the past life, and the neglected opportunity.
Every sun has to set.
Wishing does not make a poor man rich.
Dawn does not come twice to wake a man.
Do not stand in a place of danger trusting in miracles.
A chameleon does not leave one tree until it is sure of another.
Ask the experienced rather than the learned.
He who peeps through the keyhole may lose his eye.
The fruit of silence is tranquility.
Your tongue is your horse — if you take care of it, it takes care of you; if you betray it, it betrays you.
What is learned in youth is carved in stone.
The hand of God is with the community.
He who laughs last did not get the joke.
The eye cannot rise above the eyebrow.
Live together like brothers, and do business like strangers.
If speech is silver, then silence is gold.
The dogs bark, but the caravan moves on.
The ego is a veil between humans and God.
Yesterday I was clever, so I wanted to change the world. Today I am wise, so I am changing myself.
The wound is the place where the light enters you.
Let yourself be silently drawn by the strange pull of what you really love.
Where there is ruin, there is hope for a treasure.
The lion is most handsome when looking for food.
Do not be satisfied with the stories that come before you. Unfold your own myth.
You were born with wings, why prefer to crawl through life?
Raise your words, not your voice. It is rain that grows flowers, not thunder.
Travel brings power and love back into your life.
Be like a tree and let the dead leaves drop.
Whoever knows himself knows his Lord.
The ink of the scholar is more sacred than the blood of the martyr.
Do not let your difficulties fill you with anxiety; after all, it is only in the darkest nights that stars shine more brightly.
A person's true wealth is the good they do in this world.
Knowledge enlivens the soul.
People are enemies of what they do not know.
Detachment is not that you own nothing, but that nothing owns you.
The tongue is like a lion. If you let it loose, it will wound someone.
Be like the flower that gives its fragrance even to the hand that crushes it.
A moment of patience in a moment of anger saves a thousand moments of regret.
The best form of devotion to the service of God is not to make a show of it.
He who busies himself with the faults of others shall never correct his own.
Two things define you: your patience when you have nothing, and your attitude when you have everything.
Do not raise your children the way your parents raised you; they were born for a different time.
The richest of the rich is the one who is not a prisoner of greed.
Do not feel lonely on the road of righteousness because of the fewness of those who walk it.
The heart was created for the remembrance of God.
Patience is of two kinds: patience over what pains you, and patience against what you covet.
Sins are like chains and locks preventing their perpetrator from roaming the vast garden of faith.
The key to everything is the remembrance of God. The door of everything is gratitude.
Truly in the heart there is a void that cannot be filled except with the company of God.
Had God lifted the veil for His servant and shown him how He handles his affairs, his heart would have melted out of love for God.
This world is like a shadow. If you try to catch it, you will never be able to do so. If you turn your back towards it, it has no choice but to follow you.
Whoever is not grateful for small blessings will not be grateful for great ones.
The heart becomes sick as the body does, and its remedy is repentance. It becomes rusty as a mirror does, and its clarity is found in remembrance.
Knowledge without action is vanity, and action without knowledge is insanity.
The happiness of the drop is to die in the river.
Do not fix your eyes on others' faults; fix them on your own.
A thankful person is thankful under all circumstances.
Half of disbelief in God comes from a scholar who has no manners.
To get what you love, you must first be patient with what you hate.
The hypocrite looks for faults; the believer looks for excuses.
If those who do not possess knowledge avoid scholarly discussions, disagreement will end.
Work for your terrestrial life in proportion to your location in it, and work for your afterlife in proportion to your eternity in it.
The soul should take care of the body, just as the pilgrim on his way to Mecca takes care of his camel.
God is beautiful and He loves beauty.
When the world pushes you to your knees, you are in the perfect position to pray.
Every child of Adam makes mistakes, and the best of those who make mistakes are those who repent.
Take account of yourselves before you are called to account.
The most beloved of people to God are those who are most beneficial to others.
God does not look at your appearance or wealth, but rather at your hearts and deeds.
Whoever treads a path seeking knowledge, God will make easy for him the path to paradise.
We have not been given the power to change anyone; only God changes hearts.
Do not lose hope, nor be sad.
With every hardship comes ease.
The strong person is not the one who can wrestle someone else down. The strong person is the one who can control himself when he is angry.
Speak a good word or remain silent.
None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
I have never regretted my silence. As for my speech, I have regretted it many times.
May God have mercy on the one who shows me my faults.
Do not be fooled by one who recites well, for it is merely words that come from the tongue. Rather look to those who act upon it.
Acquire knowledge and teach it to the people.
Hold yourself accountable before you are held accountable, and weigh your deeds before they are weighed for you.
The less one speaks of his greatness, the more shall we think of it.
Without knowledge, action is useless; and knowledge without action is futile.
Good intentions are the most beautiful of secrets.
If an ignorant person is attracted by the things of the world, that is understandable. But if a learned person is thus attracted, it is truly strange.
Piety is not in the worship, but piety is in the character.
Be grateful for every blessing and guard against losing it by ingratitude.
O man, you are busy working for the world, and the world is busy trying to turn you out.
A believer is a mirror to his brother; when he sees a fault in him, he corrects it.
Beware of this world, for it is sweet and green, and soon you will depart from it.
The fool speaks about what he knows, and the wise man knows about what he speaks.
If you desire to be treated with mercy, then have mercy upon those who are under your authority.
Whoever fears God, God will make a way out for him from every difficulty.
The one who knows God most is the one who fears Him the most.
What can my enemies do to me? My paradise is in my heart; it goes with me wherever I go.
When a calamity strikes, the servant who is patient gains the reward of gratitude.
Repel evil with that which is better. Then the one who hated you will become your close friend.
The real imprisonment is the imprisonment of the heart, and the real freedom is the freedom of the heart.
People worship God in hope and fear. The elite worship Him in love and gratitude.
Knowledge is not what is memorized. Knowledge is what benefits.
Whoever does not love knowledge will not succeed in it.
My heart is at ease knowing that what was meant for me will never miss me, and what misses me was never meant for me.
Be humble, for the greatest things in nature are done by humble means: the dew, the rain, the sun.
I never debated with a knowledgeable person except that I won the debate, and I never debated with an ignorant person except that I lost.
If you cannot say something good, then stay silent.
When you advise someone, do so privately. Public advice is a form of humiliation.
Your love for something blinds you and makes you deaf.
The one who does not appreciate the rose will not appreciate the thorn.
A heart that sits behind lips of gold is better than a heart that sits behind lips of clay.
If you dig a pit for others, you may fall into it yourself.
He who burns his tongue on soup blows on yogurt.
Patience is a tree whose root is bitter but its fruit is sweet.
A gentle hand may lead even an elephant by a hair.
There is a way from heart to heart.
The wise make proverbs, and fools repeat them.
Thinking well is wise; planning well is wiser; doing well is wisest and best of all.
Courteous asking opens even iron doors.
Every river has its own course.
No matter how far you have gone on the wrong road, turn back.
A kind word warms for three winters.
Good actions are a guard against the blows of adversity.
He who sows courtesy reaps friendship.
Measure your quilt to your bed.
The hand that gives is better than the hand that takes.
Who digs too deep for a fish may come out with a serpent.
Only a fool tests the depth of the water with both feet.
Set your course by the stars, not by the lights of every passing ship.
Love all and trust few. Everything is real but not everything is true.
Respond to every call that excites your spirit.
Ignore those that make you fearful and sad, that degrade you back towards disease and death.
Sell your cleverness and buy bewilderment.
Silence is the language of God; all else is poor translation.
In each moment the fire rages, it will burn away a hundred veils.
What you seek is seeking you.
The garden of the world has no limits, except in your mind.
When you go through a hard period, when everything seems to oppose you, know that God is rearranging things in your favor.
Be in this world as though you were a stranger or a traveler.
The best among you are those who have the best character.
Richness is not having many possessions. Rather, true richness is the richness of the soul.
A white day with someone is not enough to judge him; but a dark night with him is more than enough.
Dwell not upon your weariness, your strength shall be according to the measure of your desire.
He who reveals his hardship will always find someone to help him.
Lower your voice and strengthen your argument.
The deeper the roots, the taller the tree.
He who has no past has no future.
Do for this life as if you live forever, do for the afterlife as if you die tomorrow.
God will not change the condition of a people until they change what is in themselves.
The most complete believer in faith is the one with the best moral character.
Every soul shall taste death.
Who is the most favored of God? He from whom the greatest good comes to His creatures.
Give glad tidings and do not drive people away.
Forgive him who wrongs you; join him who cuts you off; do good to him who does evil to you.
Leave what makes you doubt for that which does not make you doubt.
The believer is not bitten from the same hole twice.
Make things easy and do not make them difficult. Give glad tidings and do not repel people.
He who eats alone chokes alone.
The one who guards his tongue guards his life.
The moon does not heed the barking of dogs.
If the wind will not serve, take to the oars.
I complained I had no shoes until I met a man who had no feet.
He who has a thousand friends has not a friend to spare, and he who has one enemy will meet him everywhere.
If poverty were a man, I would kill him.
Protect your tongue as you protect your gold and silver.
A person who truly knows God fills every moment with something beneficial.
He who knows himself knows his Lord.
God sleeps in minerals, awakens in plants, walks in animals, and thinks in man.
The self is an ocean without a shore. Gazing upon it has no beginning or end.
My heart has become capable of every form: it is a pasture for gazelles, a monastery for monks.
Fear him who fears not God.
The gem cannot be polished without friction, nor man perfected without trials.
You will not attain righteousness until you give of that which you love.
The one who walks with the wise becomes wise, but a companion of fools will suffer harm.
Generosity is not giving me that which I need more than you do, but giving me that which you need more than I do.
The one who advises you has indeed helped you.
A candle loses nothing by lighting another candle.
Coffee should be black as hell, strong as death, and sweet as love.
The faith of a person cannot be complete until he loves for others what he loves for himself.
A drop of rain makes no distinction between the just and the unjust.
